lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Hello [player name]. How's the adventuring going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Ali. Not bad. How are you?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Still selling.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything interesting?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: For you, always. As a matter of fact I have just acquired a fleet of magic carpets, which operate throughout the Kharidian desert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that sounds great, how about your stall?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Trade is good, but I would like to expand and diversify, but it's proving a little more difficult than I initially thought.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I think the other merchants in this town are conspiring against me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh come on! You're just being paranoid now.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well maybe I can't get any new suppliers then because I'm Pollnivnian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're what?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: From Pollnivneach.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could help you then?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Perhaps you could.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: My friend in Sophanem... Ahh never mind him, maybe we might have a look at him another time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anyone else you want me to talk to on your behalf?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: There's a blackjack seller in Pollnivneach, a cautious type of guy, not the usual entrepreneurial sort that I would normally deal with, but...

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I think that he has a product with real potential...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? .... Blackjacks?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Yes, but at the moment the product isn't polished enough, if you could convince him to create a larger variety of weapon I would gladly stock his products.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's in this for me?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I thought you wanted to help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course, but everyone seems to be benefiting here but me.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well the trader gets their product sold, you get your cut, but what do I get?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: You get access to more products. Is that not reward enough?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Hmmm... Perhaps not.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I'll tell you what then, how about I let you have discounted flights on my magic carpet rides?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow that would be great.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Fair enough so, we have a win, win, win situation on our hands then.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Didn't we also agree that you would get discounted travel on my magic carpet network?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh ya, I forgot.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: So what's your problem then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have one I guess.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Good, that's what I like to hear. Now where were we?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well you had better get going, you've a fair bit of travelling ahead of you. Unless you want to make a small purchase from me first?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm really too busy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok I'll get on to it.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I have an old friend - a cloth merchant Siamun, who lives in Sophanem - Do you know the place?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm..... Images of cats and priests come to mind. Ah yes! There we go, it's the city of the dead South of Pollnivneach isn't it?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: That's the one, well if you could convince him to supply me with some of his merchandise, I would be more than willing to sell them to the masses ...... for a small percentage of course.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I think I need just one more product type, something magical ... now what could I stock that's magical, has large demand and a pretty good profit margin...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How about runes?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Yes, that would be perfect, but who could I get that would supply? There really seems to be a gap in the market in the desert but I don't know anyone in that line of business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on, I know somebody called Aubury, decent fella. I helped him out a while back. Perhaps I could approach him about the idea.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Oh yes, the rune seller from Varrock. That would be perfect. He's far enough away that he wouldn't end up competing with himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well the rune market is a limited one, if I were to sell his runes from a location nearby his shop, then I would take part of his market share so he would in fact not benefit from me selling runes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah ha I think I know where this is going. So because you're located far enough away from him you will take up someone else's share in the market and Aubury will make more money as a result.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Very well anticipated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well you had better get going, you've a fair bit of travelling ahead of you. Unless you want to make a small purchase from me first?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm really too busy.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Hello [player name]. How's the adventuring going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Ali. Not bad. How are you?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Still selling.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: ~Sigh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's up with you?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well, I always fancied that I could be a great draper, but the way things are looking that's not about to happen any time soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I could do to help?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I have a friend - well, actually more of an acquaintance - who lives in Sophanem, some way to the south of here, and who may be able to help. But, from what I've heard, no one can enter or leave the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could give it a look.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Is there any chance of getting you to look up a merchant to supply me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who did you want me to talk to again?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Go see Siamun in Sophanem about some clothes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, so I'll go see Siamun of Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Good, good.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Could you talk to your friend Aubury in Varrock about supplying me with runes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, that could work out quite well. Varrock is always quite busy, so I might try to get a couple of other things done while I'm there.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Don't tarry there too long, I'm quite anxious to get a rune store open before anyone else in this area.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: In Pollnivneach go and talk to the blackjack seller. Convince him to supply me with his produce.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any ideas how I should go about that?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: You're a bright chap. I'm sure you'll work something out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get to it right away.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Is there any chance of getting you to look up a merchant to supply me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ya, I'll get to it soon.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well you had better get going, you've a fair bit of travelling ahead of you. Unless you want to make a small purchase from me first?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm really too busy.

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Do you want to buy some runes?

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Oh, sorry [player name], I didn't recognise you for a second, you're back again so soon. Have you spoken to Ali Morrisane yet about the runes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm... not yet, I'll try to get around to doing that soon.

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Great! Now, do want to buy some runes?

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Do you want to buy some runes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm here with a business proposal from Ali Morrisane.

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Ali who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ali Morrisane, a salesman of great repute based in Al Kharid.

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: What is this proposal then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He wants to set up a rune-selling franchise in the markets of Al Kharid and he wants you to supply him with the stock.

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Why doesn't he get his own runes and sell them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: His talents don't lie in that area. What he's good at is selling things, and lots of them.

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Hmm, interesting... So let's get this straight, he wants me to supply him with runes, which he will sell, and send me the profits?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nearly. He will charge a small commission on the runes.

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Do you think that it will work? Will people actually buy these runes off him?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I wouldn't be surprised. Supply and demand are his rune essence and ore.

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Now this is very important, is he trustworthy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I believe so. Trust is a key part of commerce and something, which I doubt he would neglect.

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Fair enough, I'll trust your judgement.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll supply him with runes then?

lines.speaker: Aubury
lines.text: Well if he's half as good a salesman as you suggest I should have no problem in funding my research and maybe even redecorating this place.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Hello [player name]. How's the adventuring going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Ali. Not bad. How are you?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Still selling.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: ~Sigh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's up with you?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well, I always fancied that I could be a great draper, but the way things are looking that's not about to happen any time soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I could do to help?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I have a friend - well, actually more of an acquaintance - who lives in Sophanem, some way to the south of here, and who may be able to help. But, from what I've heard, no one can enter or leave the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could give it a look.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Good job on getting the rune shipments sorted. I'm just receiving the first of them. Though there seems to be a funny sort of locking mechanism on them, which I haven't completely figured out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem. I might have a look at it later.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Hang on a second.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well at the moment I don't exactly have a selection of runes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Those boxes from your friend Aubury have a very odd locking mechanism.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have managed to get them off?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Not exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean by 'not exactly'?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: What I mean by 'not exactly' is 'no, I haven't managed to get the lock off'.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I was kind of hoping that you would have a look at them. There are two distinctive types of caskets one with a simple locking mechanism and another, which ummm... looks a little tricky to say the least.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll have a go at opening a small casket of runes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm feeling lucky, let's have a look at opening one of those large caskets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm... This lock looks like it may be impossible to prise open.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmmm... This lock looks like it may be impossible to pick open.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On second thoughts I don't really need any runes.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Word has it that you're trying to get the blackjack man to change his kit.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you know that?

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Hey there I heard that you were trying to get the blackjack man to change his gear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You did?

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Do you forget who you're talking to? I'm the fastest and sneakiest urchin in this town.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, so what's it to you then?

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Well it suits my purpose nearly as much as yours if the blackjack seller produces a wider variety of wares.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How so?

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Well chances are, he'll produce some even higher quality items for me to steal

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aha so if you can rob the blackjack seller and get away with it, it should prove to him that his blackjack isn't up to the job?

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: There's hope for you yet.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Seeing as we've worked successfully together in the past I will only charge you 100 gold.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Seeing as you owe me from the last bit of work we did together this is going to cost you 300 gold.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have that much money on me.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Pity, maybe we can do it again sometime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Woah! That's far too much.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough, here you go.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Now walk up to the merchant, engage him in conversation and leave the rest up to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I'm back.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: What is it now?

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Yoink!

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Hey come back you thief!

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Hello. Could I interest you in a Blackjack?

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: That pesky little urchin has done it again! I can't believe it he's managed to rob me again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well maybe you should modify the blackjack to have better defensive qualities.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: I might as well. Next time I'll get him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I take it you will be supplying Ali Morrisane with the new variety of blackjack then?

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Yes, yes. I'll start shipping it by carpet as soon as the first batch is ready.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Hello. Could I interest you in a Blackjack?

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Oh sorry Player. I mistook you for another customer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No problem.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Have you checked with Ali Morrisane whether my blackjacks are selling or not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, I have a few thing to do first.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Well I would be very grateful if you could have a talk with him sooner rather than later, I am still a little nervous about the new variation of my blackjack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Hah! Did you see the face on the blackjack seller! Ha boyo! I had to move quick to get out of the way of his blackjack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You did well. I thought he hit you when swung his blackjack at you.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Ya he nearly did. Boyo, does my head still hurt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You mean he hit you?

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Barely, the trick is in moving with the blow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I might bear that in mind for next time.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Fantastic job on the blackjacks. I started getting the first batches of them a short time ago, and they've being flying off the shelves, although the demand seems to be dropping off a little.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not too badly, I hope.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: No, no, they're still selling fine, but if you could perhaps convince the blackjack man to send a different variety the next time you see him, I would be more than happy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should like to have a look at them, at some stage.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Hello. Could I interest you in a Blackjack?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I interest you in a proposal?

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: What are we talking about here? Nothing dodgy I hope. I don't like anything dodgy, I had enough of that sort of thing when the bandits and those rogue Menaphites were running things here, so I packed up my things and left.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No no nothing of the sort, a friend of mine Ali Morrisane, have you ever heard of him?

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Have I what? He's my idol, he's the greatest salesman in all of Gielinor. Word has it that he even sold...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...yeah yeah, sand to the Bedabin. Hmpf!! I have heard that story more times that I care for.. Anyway he has a proposal for you...

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: I'm all ears. Does he want to take me on as an apprentice?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No no nothing of the sort.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He sees quite a bit of potential in your product.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: The urchins of the town do too, they're always trying to steal my products.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but Ali says if he were to stock your products he would need a larger variation of product.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: I don't know. I'm quite happy with the product that I'm making at the moment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm.. Ali did voice some concerns about you. He said you were an over cautious individual and ...

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: He did?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look will you create a larger variety of blackjacks or not?

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I have to do to convince you that you should make a larger variety?

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Well I suppose if you could demonstrate to me that they weren't up to the job.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I'll have to think of a cunning plan.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was what? Oh I must have been thinking out loud again, sorry.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Psst... Player Come here.

lines.speaker: Street urchin
lines.text: Psst... Player Come here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I bet you couldn't knock me out with your blackjack.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: I reckon I could.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll tell you what, if you can't knock me out with one blow, you should make better blackjacks.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Nah, I don't like the sounds of that. It leaves a lot up to chance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, ok, how about two blows to the head?

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Are you sure? I could possibly kill you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm... on second thoughts I might give it a skip. I just had my hair done, I wouldn't want to mess it up.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Phew! Sighs with relief. At last the voice of reason speaks.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Now if you don't mind I'll be getting back to work. Thank you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure. No pain no gain. Grrr!

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: You adventurers are crazy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow! Ha! You didn't knock me down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pah, you didn't even draw blood.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeeeoouch that hurt! I'm still standing though You can't knock me down.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: You really are truly mad.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you going to hit me again? I really want to get this over with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow! Ha! You didn't knock me down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pah, you didn't even draw blood.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeeeoouch that hurt! I'm still standing though You can't knock me down.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Either your head is thicker than it looks or else I need to rework my blackjack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm... definitely the blackjack. You just about fight off another bout of dizziness and nausea.

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: I suppose I'll just have to modify the blackjack so that it has a better attack.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great, so I take it you will be supplying Ali Morrisane with the new variety of blackjack then?

lines.speaker: Blackjack seller
lines.text: Yes, yes. I'll start shipping it by carpet as soon as the first batch is ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Siamun?

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you are Siamun then?

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Yes now what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, are you this friendly to everyone?

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Just you. Now what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A mutual friend of ours, Ali Morrisane, wants you to supply his stall in Al Kharid with your garments.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Now why would I do that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well for one, you'd probably sell more clothes and once the people of Al Kharid see your amazing works of tailoring, I'm sure they'd spread the word of your clothes far and wide.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Interesting, but I would need more materials to accomplish what you ask of me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well than what do you need?

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Either get me 3 pots of dye, or 3 animal pelts of the same variety and whatever you do, don't bring me banknotes!

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Hello adventurer. How are sales going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not too bad, Ali was well impressed by the reaction and demand they had initially but he's a little concerned about how the sales have dropped off of recent.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Hmmm... Maybe I could produce a different range of products.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: I'll tell you what, get me 3 pots of dye and I'll change the type of garments I'm sending to Ali and, remember, I won't accept banknotes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll get you the materials.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Unless you've got the materials on you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ummm.. No.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Keh! Come back when you have what I need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm... What do you need again?

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Three pots of dye, no banknotes. Can you remember that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Good, good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, I've got what you want with me.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Thank you adventurer. I will get my goods up to that merchant thief - Ali Morrisane as quickly as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Think nothing of it. I'm looking forward to seeing what you've produced for the Al Kharid market.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Siamun.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Hello adventurer. How are sales going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not too bad, Ali was well impressed by the reaction and demand they had initially but he's a little concerned about how the sales have dropped off of recent.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Hmmm... Maybe I could produce a different range of products.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: I tell you what, get me 3 animal pelts of the same variety and I'll change the shipments around and, remember, I won't accept banknotes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean by animal pelt?

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Just get me 3 cowhides, 3 wolf or snake skins or the shearings of 3 sheep. Simpler still get me 3 pots of dye and, remember, I won't accept banknotes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll get you the materials.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Unless you've got the materials on you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ummm.. No.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Keh! Come back when you have what I need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Umm... What do you need again?

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Just get me 3 cowhides, 3 wolf or snake skins or the shearings of 3 sheep and, remember, I won't accept banknotes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, I've got what you want with me.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Thank you adventurer. I will get my goods up to that merchant thief - Ali Morrisane as quickly as possible.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Think nothing of it. I'm looking forward to seeing what you've produced for the Al Kharid market.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, I'll get you the materials.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Unless you've got the materials on you?

sections.title: After giving the dyes or animal pelts

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Hello adventurer. Have you checked with Ali M whether my garments are selling or not?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet, I have a few things to do first.

lines.speaker: Siamun
lines.text: Well I would be very much obliged it you could have a talk with him sooner rather than later, I'm not all that sure of the trustworthiness of those carpet men.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll get to it.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Siamun's clothes seem to have really struck a chord with the locals in Al Kharid. Menaphite culture seems to be taking off here, rumour has it that one of Prince Ali's servants bought him one of Siamun's robes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that sounds positive.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Quite, if I don't say so myself. But in order to keep things fresh, the next time you're in Sophanem, could you ask that cranky old so-and-so Siamun to produce a new line of clothing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, why not. The Sultan himself might even buy some of the new range.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: That would be the day.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: [Player name], I would like to commend you on all the selfless help you have given me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It was nothing, really.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I think you underestimate your efforts. Anyway, as a token of my gratitude I have told all my magic carpet attendants to allow you to travel on the carpets at a discounted rate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really, Ali, there is no need.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I insist.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Hello [player name]. How's the adventuring going?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi Ali. Not bad. How are you?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Still selling.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear you're planning to expand your business here...

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Of course, my friend. Any good salesman ought to take the opportunities that present themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But your men are threatening the other shopkeepers!

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: That is regrettable, I'm afraid.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And the gnomes don't sound very happy about your magic carpet business.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: A good businessman looks for all the opportunities he can.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aren't you going to do anything about your men?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well, these associates of my nephew can be somewhat... overenthusiastic.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: I'm sure they'll settle down soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But they're threatening to hurt the shopkeepers if they don't cooperate!

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Well, there's a simple solution there, don't you agree?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you planning to take over all of Al Kharid's shops?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: It's a step in the right direction.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Now, would you like to have a look at my wares?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm really too busy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will you do about the gnomes?

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: They can continue running their gliders into trees, and I'll keep my business going. Sooner or later it'll all work out in my favour.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I suppose so...

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Anyway, before you go, take a look at my wares.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, good luck with your business.

lines.speaker: Ali Morrisane
lines.text: Before you go, have a look at my wares.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I'm really too busy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.
